enrichment.short_description: Eleventy (11ty) is a free, open-source static site generator that compiles templates into fast, JavaScript-free HTML.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Eleventy is a free open-source static site generator supporting 13+ template languages with zero-config, fast builds.

features.title: Multiple template languages
features.description: Supports HTML, Markdown, WebC, JavaScript, Liquid, Nunjucks, Handlebars, Mustache, EJS, Haml, Pug, TypeScript, JSX, and MDX in the same project.

features.title: Fast builds
features.description: Compiles large sites quickly, with published benchmarks showing 4,000 Markdown files built in under 2 seconds.

features.title: No client-side JavaScript by default
features.description: Eleventy ships plain HTML output with no required JavaScript framework or client-side runtime.

features.title: Zero-config start
features.description: Works out of the box with no required configuration file, while still offering flexible configuration options.

features.title: Works with existing directory structures
features.description: Designed for incremental adoption into existing projects rather than requiring a specific folder layout.

pros: Free and open source with no vendor lock-in
Fast build performance on large sites
Supports many template languages side by side in one project
Ships no JavaScript to the browser by default
cons: Requires Node.js 18 or newer to run
No official hosted/cloud product; deployment must be configured separately with a host of your choice
As a build tool rather than an application, it has no built-in UI for non-developers

faqs.question: Is Eleventy free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, Eleventy is open source and free to use.

faqs.question: What template languages does Eleventy support?
faqs.answer: 13+ languages, including Markdown, Liquid, Nunjucks, Handlebars, EJS, Pug, JSX, and MDX.

faqs.question: Does Eleventy require Node.js?
faqs.answer: Yes, Eleventy requires Node.js 18 or newer and can be installed via npm, pnpm, or yarn.

faqs.question: Who maintains Eleventy?
faqs.answer: Eleventy was originally created by Zach Leat and is now part of the Font Awesome organization following an acquisition in September 2024.

use_cases.title: Static blogs and marketing sites
use_cases.description: Compile Markdown and template content into pre-rendered HTML pages without a JavaScript framework.

use_cases.title: Documentation sites
use_cases.description: Generate documentation output from Markdown and templates with fast rebuild times.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Eleventy?
paragraphs: Eleventy, also known as 11ty, is an open-source static site generator that compiles template files into a pre-rendered `_site` output folder. It supports 13+ template languages side by side, including Markdown, Liquid, Nunjucks, Handlebars, and JSX, and ships no client-side JavaScript by default.

The project was originally created by Zach Leat and is now maintained under the Font Awesome organization following a September 2024 acquisition. Eleventy is used across a large number of public GitHub repositories for blogs, documentation, and marketing sites.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Eleventy is for
paragraphs: Eleventy suits developers who want a fast, framework-agnostic static site generator that can be adopted incrementally into an existing project without requiring a full JavaScript framework stack.

enrichment.short_description: Nuxt is a free, open-source full-stack Vue.js framework with file-based routing, SSR/SSG, and a 200+ module ecosystem.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Nuxt is an open-source, MIT-licensed Vue framework offering file-based routing, SSR/SSG, and 200+ modules for full-stack apps.

features.title: File-based routing
features.description: Routes and auto-imports are configured automatically based on project file structure.

features.title: Multiple rendering modes
features.description: Supports server-side rendering (SSR), client-side rendering (CSR), and static-site generation (SSG) in the same framework.

features.title: Built on Vue, Vite, and Nitro
features.description: Uses Vue.js for the frontend, Vite as the bundler, and the Nitro server engine for the backend.

features.title: Built-in TypeScript support
features.description: Ships with TypeScript support and automatically generated types.

features.title: Modular architecture
features.description: Over 200 community modules extend Nuxt with additional functionality.

features.title: SEO and meta tag management
features.description: Includes built-in tooling for managing meta tags and SEO-related settings.

pros: Free, open source, and MIT licensed
Large module ecosystem with 200+ available extensions
Supports SSR, SSG, and CSR from a single framework
Backed by sponsors including Vercel
cons: Requires familiarity with Vue.js to use effectively
No dedicated managed pricing page was found describing paid enterprise support on the reviewed site content

faqs.question: Is Nuxt free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, Nuxt is 100% open source under the MIT License.

faqs.question: What rendering modes does Nuxt support?
faqs.answer: Server-side rendering (SSR), client-side rendering (CSR), and static-site generation (SSG).

faqs.question: What does Nuxt use under the hood?
faqs.answer: Vue.js for the frontend, Vite as the bundler, and Nitro as the server engine.

faqs.question: Who uses Nuxt?
faqs.answer: Nuxt's site lists enterprises such as Louis Vuitton, Blizzard, and NASA as users, alongside smaller startups.

use_cases.title: Server-rendered Vue applications
use_cases.description: Build SSR Vue.js apps with file-based routing and automatic imports.

use_cases.title: Static and hybrid sites
use_cases.description: Generate statically rendered or hybrid-rendered sites using Nuxt's SSG mode.
use_cases.audience: Teams building marketing or content sites on Vue

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Nuxt?
paragraphs: Nuxt is an open-source, full-stack framework for Vue.js that provides file-based routing, automatic auto-imports, and multiple rendering modes (SSR, CSR, and SSG). It is built on Vue.js, Vite, and the Nitro server engine.

Nuxt is 100% open source under the MIT License and has a modular architecture with more than 200 community-built modules covering things like content management, image optimization, and authentication.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Nuxt is for
paragraphs: Nuxt is aimed at Vue.js developers who want an opinionated, batteries-included framework for building production web applications, from small projects to large-scale enterprise sites.

enrichment.short_description: SvelteKit is a free, open-source framework for building web applications with Svelte, offering routing, SSR, and SSG.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: SvelteKit is an open-source, MIT-licensed framework for building Svelte web apps with routing, SSR, SSG, and deployment adapters.

features.title: Filesystem-based routing
features.description: Routes update the UI automatically when links are clicked, based on the project's file structure.

features.title: Server-side rendering and static generation
features.description: Supports rendering parts of an app on the server (SSR) or prerendering at build time (SSG).

features.title: Adapter-based deployment
features.description: Deploys to Node servers, Cloudflare, Netlify, and Vercel through a configurable adapter system.

features.title: Built-in image optimization
features.description: Includes tooling for optimizing images as part of the build process.

features.title: Page preloading
features.description: Fetches pages before the user navigates to them for faster perceived performance.

features.title: Offline support
features.description: Provides service worker functionality for offline use cases.

features.title: Vite-powered dev experience
features.description: Uses Vite with Svelte plugins for hot module replacement and fast local development.

pros: Free and open source under the MIT license
Flexible deployment via adapters for multiple hosting platforms
Fast development experience powered by Vite
Built-in SSR, SSG, and CSR support
cons: Smaller ecosystem and community than React- or Vue-based frameworks
Requires learning Svelte's compiler-based component model

faqs.question: Is SvelteKit free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, SvelteKit is part of the Svelte ecosystem, which is free and open source under the MIT license.

faqs.question: What rendering modes does SvelteKit support?
faqs.answer: Server-side rendering (SSR), client-side rendering (CSR), and static-site generation (SSG).

faqs.question: How does SvelteKit handle deployment?
faqs.answer: Through a configurable adapter system supporting Node servers, Cloudflare, Netlify, and Vercel.

faqs.question: What build tool does SvelteKit use?
faqs.answer: SvelteKit uses Vite for development and building.

use_cases.title: Full-featured web applications
use_cases.description: Build routed, server-rendered applications using Svelte components.

use_cases.title: Statically generated sites
use_cases.description: Prerender pages at build time for static hosting.
use_cases.audience: Teams building content or marketing sites with Svelte

long_form_blocks.heading: What is SvelteKit?
paragraphs: SvelteKit is a framework for building web applications using Svelte, comparable in role to Next.js for React or Nuxt for Vue. It provides filesystem-based routing, SSR, SSG, and CSR, plus an adapter system for deploying to Node, Cloudflare, Netlify, and Vercel.
It is part of the Svelte project, which is free and open source under the MIT license, and is built on top of Vite for development tooling.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who SvelteKit is for
paragraphs: SvelteKit is for developers who want to build performant web applications with Svelte and need routing, rendering, and deployment handled by a cohesive framework rather than assembled from separate tools.

enrichment.short_description: Remix is an open-source, full-stack JavaScript web framework maintained by Shopify, combining server, router, and data layer in one dependency.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Remix is a free, MIT-licensed full-stack JavaScript framework from Shopify with a built-in server, router, and data layer.

features.title: Full-stack in one dependency
features.description: Bundles a server, router, data layer, UI components, and testing tools into a single framework dependency.

features.title: Web-standard components
features.description: UI components are built on web primitives such as EventTarget rather than being tied to React-only hooks.

features.title: Interoperability
features.description: Designed to work with web components and third-party libraries.

features.title: AI-agent-friendly design
features.description: The framework is positioned to work with AI coding agents and plain-language modifications.

pros: Free and open source under the MIT license
Maintained and backed by Shopify
Combines frontend and backend concerns in a single dependency
Documentation and examples are openly licensed on GitHub
cons: Remix 3 is currently available only as a beta release
Requires adopting Remix's full-stack conventions rather than a plain client-side setup

faqs.question: Is Remix free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, Remix is open source under the MIT license.

faqs.question: Who maintains Remix?
faqs.answer: Remix is owned and maintained by Shopify, Inc.

faqs.question: Is Remix 3 stable?
faqs.answer: As of the current release, Remix 3 is available as a beta.

faqs.question: What is included in the Remix framework?
faqs.answer: A server, router, data layer, UI components, and testing tools.

use_cases.title: Full-stack JavaScript applications
use_cases.description: Build applications with server, routing, and data-fetching handled by a single framework.

use_cases.title: Rapid prototyping to production apps
use_cases.description: Scale from quick prototypes to established production applications on the same framework.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Remix?
paragraphs: Remix is an open-source, full-stack JavaScript framework that bundles a server, router, data layer, UI components, and testing tools into a single dependency. It is owned and maintained by Shopify, Inc.

Remix's components are built on web-standard primitives such as EventTarget rather than being exclusively tied to React hooks, and the framework is designed to interoperate with web components and third-party libraries. Remix 3 is currently available as a beta release.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Remix is for
paragraphs: Remix targets developers who want a single, cohesive full-stack framework covering routing, data loading, and UI rendering, from quick prototypes through to production applications.

enrichment.short_description: Qwik is an open-source web framework that uses resumability to skip hydration and make applications instantly interactive.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Qwik is a free, MIT-licensed web framework that uses resumability to skip hydration for instantly interactive apps.

features.title: Resumability
features.description: Skips traditional hydration so applications become instantly interactive, rather than re-executing app logic on the client.

features.title: JSX syntax
features.description: Uses JSX and functional components, targeting developers already familiar with React.

features.title: Built-in tooling
features.description: Includes Vite for development and Vitest and Playwright for testing.

features.title: Styling support
features.description: Works with Tailwind and PostCSS for styling.

features.title: SSR and prerendering
features.description: Supports server-side rendering and prerendering with debugging tools.

pros: Free and open source under the MIT license
Resumability avoids the hydration cost typical of other frameworks
JSX syntax is familiar to developers coming from React
Sponsored by Builder.io
cons: Smaller community and ecosystem compared to React or Vue
Newer framework with fewer third-party integrations and less production track record

faqs.question: Is Qwik free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, Qwik is open source under the MIT License.

faqs.question: What is resumability in Qwik?
faqs.answer: A technique that skips hydration so applications become interactive immediately, rather than waiting for client-side JavaScript to re-execute.

faqs.question: Who sponsors Qwik?
faqs.answer: Builder.io is listed as a special sponsor funding the project's development.

faqs.question: What testing tools does Qwik include?
faqs.answer: Vitest and Playwright are included as part of the standard tooling.

use_cases.title: High-performance web apps
use_cases.description: Build applications that need to be interactive immediately without a hydration delay.

use_cases.title: React-to-Qwik migrations
use_cases.description: Adopt Qwik using familiar JSX and functional-component patterns.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Qwik?
paragraphs: Qwik is an open-source web framework built around resumability, a technique that skips traditional hydration so applications are instantly interactive. It uses JSX and functional components, and ships with Vite, Vitest, and Playwright as part of its standard tooling.
Qwik is MIT licensed and maintained by the Qwik team, with Builder.io listed as a special sponsor funding its development.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Qwik is for
paragraphs: Qwik is aimed at web developers, particularly those with React experience, who want to build high-performance applications and avoid the hydration overhead common in other JavaScript frameworks.

name: Docute
skip_reason: The GitHub repo (egoist/docute) is archived as of March 2025 and marked no longer actively maintained by its author, with the last release dating to October 2019; the official homepage (docute.org) is also unreachable (connection refused).

enrichment.short_description: Theneo is an AI-powered API documentation and developer portal platform with real-time collaboration and a free starter plan.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Theneo is an AI-powered API documentation and developer portal platform with a free plan and paid tiers from $120/month.

features.title: AI-powered documentation generation
features.description: Automatically generates documentation from API specs, plus an "Ask AI" search feature for developers.

features.title: Real-time collaboration
features.description: Live cursors and presence indicators allow simultaneous editing across teams.

features.title: Developer portals
features.description: Private, authenticated portals with per-customer access controls.

features.title: Changelog management
features.description: Smart detection of breaking changes for changelog documentation.

features.title: Markdown and WYSIWYG editors
features.description: Flexible editing options for engineers and non-technical users.

features.title: Version control
features.description: Rollback capabilities and full audit logs for documentation changes.

features.title: Multi-format API support
features.description: Supports REST, Async, SOAP, GraphQL, and gRPC API documentation.

features.title: Agent integration
features.description: Provides an MCP server and llms.txt support for AI agent compatibility.

pros: Free Starter plan available (1 public project, 2 private projects, up to 20 team members)
SOC 2 Type II and ISO 27001/9001 certified
Self-hosting available on the Enterprise plan
Supports multiple API spec formats (REST, Async, SOAP, GraphQL, gRPC)
cons: Business and Growth tiers are priced per workspace, which can add up for multiple teams
Enterprise pricing and self-hosting require a custom quote
Monthly billing is notably more expensive than annual billing across all paid tiers

faqs.question: Does Theneo have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Starter plan is free, with limits on projects (1 public, 2 private) and up to 20 team members.

faqs.question: What API formats does Theneo support?
faqs.answer: REST, Async, SOAP, GraphQL, and gRPC.

faqs.question: Can Theneo be self-hosted?
faqs.answer: Yes, self-hosting is available on the Enterprise plan.

faqs.question: Does Theneo offer AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, including AI-generated documentation from API specs and an "Ask AI" search assistant.

faqs.question: How much does Theneo's Business plan cost?
faqs.answer: $120/month per workspace billed annually, or $150/month billed monthly.

use_cases.title: API documentation for engineering teams
use_cases.description: Generate and collaboratively maintain API reference documentation across REST, GraphQL, and other formats.

use_cases.title: Partner and customer developer portals
use_cases.description: Publish private, authenticated documentation portals with per-customer access controls.
use_cases.audience: B2B platforms, fintechs, and regulated industries

features: Custom domain, branding, and design templates

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Theneo?
paragraphs: Theneo is a developer portal platform for creating and managing API documentation, changelogs, and private customer-facing portals. It combines AI-assisted documentation generation with real-time collaborative editing, and supports REST, Async, SOAP, GraphQL, and gRPC API formats.
The platform is SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, and ISO 9001 certified, and offers a free Starter plan alongside paid Business, Growth, and Enterprise tiers, with self-hosting available on Enterprise.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Theneo is for
paragraphs: Theneo is built for engineering and developer relations teams that need to produce and maintain API documentation collaboratively, including B2B platforms, fintechs, and regulated industries that require authenticated, per-customer developer portals.

enrichment.short_description: Backstage is a free, open-source developer portal framework created at Spotify for building software catalogs and internal platforms.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Backstage is an open-source developer portal framework from Spotify offering a software catalog, templates, and TechDocs.

features.title: Software Catalog
features.description: Tracks microservices, libraries, data pipelines, websites, and ML models in a central catalog.

features.title: Software Templates
features.description: Accelerates project creation while enforcing organizational standards through self-service templates.

features.title: TechDocs
features.description: Facilitates technical documentation using a docs-as-code approach.

features.title: Extensible plugin ecosystem
features.description: Supports a growing collection of community-maintained plugins for customization.

pros: Free and open source
CNCF Incubation project, graduated from Sandbox status in March 2022
Created and proven at Spotify before being open-sourced
Large, extensible plugin ecosystem
cons: Self-hosted only; requires setup and ongoing maintenance by the adopting organization
No managed/hosted offering was found on the official site content reviewed

faqs.question: Is Backstage free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Backstage is open source and free to use.

faqs.question: Who created Backstage?
faqs.answer: Backstage was created at Spotify before being open-sourced and handed to community maintenance.

faqs.question: Is Backstage a CNCF project?
faqs.answer: Yes, it graduated from CNCF Sandbox to Incubation status in March 2022.

faqs.question: What is TechDocs?
faqs.answer: A Backstage feature for producing technical documentation using a docs-as-code workflow.

use_cases.title: Building an internal developer portal
use_cases.description: Centralize software catalogs, documentation, and self-service tooling in one portal.

use_cases.title: Standardizing project creation
use_cases.description: Use Software Templates to enforce organizational standards when scaffolding new services.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Backstage?
paragraphs: Backstage is an open-source framework for building developer portals, created at Spotify and now maintained as a community project. It centralizes a Software Catalog, Software Templates, and TechDocs technical documentation into a single internal platform.
Backstage graduated from CNCF Sandbox to Incubation project status in March 2022 and has an extensible plugin ecosystem maintained by the community.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Backstage is for
paragraphs: Backstage is aimed at platform engineering teams, engineering managers, and developers who want to build a self-hosted internal developer portal that unifies service catalogs, documentation, and standardized project scaffolding.

enrichment.short_description: Port is an internal developer portal and platform engineering tool with a software catalog, AI agents, and a free plan for up to 15 seats.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Port is an internal developer portal platform with a free plan, AI agents, and paid tiers starting at $30 per seat per month.

features.title: Software Catalog
features.description: Tracks services, components, and infrastructure across an engineering organization.

features.title: Context Lake
features.description: Aggregates environmental and engineering tool data into a centralized intelligence layer.

features.title: Self-service Actions
features.description: Lets developers trigger governed, self-service workflows.

features.title: AI Agents
features.description: Auto-discovers and orchestrates agentic workflows across the engineering ecosystem.

features.title: Scorecards
features.description: Measures engineering metrics and the ROI of initiatives such as AI adoption.

features.title: Workflow Orchestrator
features.description: Automates processes across the SDLC with built-in policy enforcement.

features.title: Integration marketplace
features.description: Connects to Slack, Jira, Cursor IDE, GitHub, and 100+ other tools.

pros: Free plan for up to 15 seats and 10,000 entities with no credit card required
Published, transparent pricing for Basic and Standard tiers
Broad integration marketplace with 100+ integrations
Uptime SLA published for paid tiers (99.8% Basic/Standard, 99.9% Enterprise)
cons: Automation run limits (500) on Free and Basic plans may be restrictive at scale
SSO and dynamic permissions require the Standard plan or higher
Advanced security features (SCIM, Private Link, IP allowlisting) are Enterprise-only

faqs.question: Does Port have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, free for up to 15 seats, 10,000 entities, and 500 automation runs, with no credit card required.

faqs.question: How much does Port cost?
faqs.answer: The Basic plan starts at $30/seat/month billed annually; the Standard plan is $40/seat/month billed annually.

faqs.question: Does Port offer single sign-on?
faqs.answer: Yes, SSO and dynamic permissions are available starting on the Standard plan.

faqs.question: What is Port's uptime SLA?
faqs.answer: 99.8% on the Basic and Standard plans, and 99.9% on Enterprise.

use_cases.title: Building an internal developer portal
use_cases.description: Catalog services and infrastructure with a self-service interface for developers.

use_cases.title: Automating engineering workflows
use_cases.description: Use Actions and the Workflow Orchestrator to automate infrastructure and SDLC processes with policy enforcement.

features: SCIM provisioning, Private Link, IP allowlisting

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Port?
paragraphs: Port is an internal developer portal and platform engineering tool built around a Software Catalog, a Context Lake that aggregates engineering data, and self-service Actions. It also includes AI Agents, Scorecards, and a Workflow Orchestrator for automating processes across the software development lifecycle.
Port publishes tiered pricing: a Free plan for up to 15 seats, paid Basic and Standard plans priced per seat, and a custom Enterprise plan with unlimited seats and additional security controls.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Port is for
paragraphs: Port is designed for platform engineering, DevOps, and SRE teams that need a software catalog combined with self-service automation and governance across their engineering ecosystem.

enrichment.short_description: Cortex is an engineering operations platform offering a service catalog, scorecards, and workflow automation, priced via custom quote.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Cortex is an engineering operations platform with a service catalog, scorecards, and workflow automation, priced via custom quote.

features.title: Catalog & Context Graph
features.description: Aggregates data from engineering tools into a unified view of services and team status.

features.title: Scorecards
features.description: Tracks production readiness and AI adoption metrics across the organization.

features.title: Workflows
features.description: Automates infrastructure provisioning, service scaffolding, and migrations through standardized "golden paths."

features.title: Compliance and audit support
features.description: Provides features supporting compliance and audit requirements for engineering organizations.

pros: SOC 2 Type II and ISO/IEC 27001:2022 certified
Backed by Y Combinator
Combines service catalog, scorecards, and workflow automation in one platform
Focused specifically on production readiness and engineering operations metrics
cons: No published pricing; requires booking a demo for a custom proposal
Founded year and headquarters are not disclosed on the official site

faqs.question: Does Cortex publish pricing?
faqs.answer: No, Cortex states its pricing is customized to each team's needs via a proposal after a demo.

faqs.question: Is Cortex certified for security compliance?
faqs.answer: Yes, Cortex is SOC 2 Type II and ISO/IEC 27001:2022 certified.

faqs.question: Who backs Cortex?
faqs.answer: Cortex is a Y Combinator-backed company.

faqs.question: What does Cortex track?
faqs.answer: Production readiness and AI adoption metrics through its Scorecards feature.

use_cases.title: Tracking production readiness
use_cases.description: Use Scorecards to monitor whether services meet production-readiness standards.

use_cases.title: Automating service scaffolding
use_cases.description: Use Workflows to provision infrastructure and scaffold new services via standardized golden paths.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Cortex?
paragraphs: Cortex is an engineering operations platform that functions as an internal developer portal, providing a service catalog and context graph, production-readiness and AI-adoption Scorecards, and workflow automation through standardized "golden paths."
The platform is SOC 2 Type II and ISO/IEC 27001:2022 certified and is a Y Combinator-backed company. Pricing is not published; Cortex prepares a customized proposal after a demo.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Cortex is for
paragraphs: Cortex targets engineering leaders, platform engineering teams, and SRE teams that need to track production readiness, measure AI adoption, and automate infrastructure workflows across their organization.
